page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the ARM64 architecture, it features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the Neoverse-V2 (ARMv9) design running at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1D cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and is managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

For local data storage, the c8gd.4xlarge includes a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong memory performance, achieving a top-tier rating in the Passmark memory score and strong ratings in large-block uncached memory bandwidth reads and writes. Single-threaded gzip compression and both single- and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also rank in the top 25% of tested hardware.

The hardware profile delivers balanced performance for compute-heavy workloads that benefit from local high-speed storage. While CPU-intensive tasks like stress-ng and Redis operations perform in the average tier, the instance excels in database operations and text processing. This makes the c8gd.4xlarge appropriate for distributed databases, data compression pipelines, web serving, and containerized microservices requiring low-latency local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by the 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and includes a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory performance, with strong ratings in large-block memory bandwidth, data decompression, and database operations. The instance provides high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for ARM-native deployments. It is designed for workloads requiring a balance of dedicated compute power and high-speed local storage, such as distributed databases, text processing, and data compression pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory and decompression performance.

32.0 GB of system memory with top-tier Passmark memory benchmark performance
